storage_extensions.usage: mmap line_int16.bin; read as int16_t per row instead of original line.bin (int8_t). Treat line==0 as NULL (excluded from AVG). All other values 1-482 are valid line numbers. Row order matches pre/adsh_code.bin exactly.

filters.note: null_stmt_code is code for empty string '' from indexes/stmt_codes.bin; sentinel -99 means accept all

data_structures.aggregation: per-thread direct array cnt[n_groups], sum_line[n_groups], cnt_line[n_groups] indexed by group_id (0..13); tiny — fits in L1 cache
data_structures.distinct_adsh: shared atomic bitset: n_groups × ceil(86135/64) uint64_t words = 14 × 1347 × 8 bytes = 150KB total; threads do fetch_or(word, mask, relaxed) to set bits; no per-thread bitsets — eliminates 43MB per-thread allocation overhead
data_structures.group_map: flat uint8_t[256][256] precomputed at startup for (stmt_code, rfile_code) → group_id; 0xFF = invalid

parallelism.per_thread_state: cnt[14] + sum_line[14] + cnt_line[14] — 336 bytes total, always cache-resident
parallelism.shared_state: atomic_bitset[14][1347] for COUNT(DISTINCT adsh) — 150KB, fits in L2/LLC

optimization_notes: Root cause (correctness): the original line.bin stores int8_t, which truncates line values >= 128 via C signed overflow — (int8_t)300 = 44, (int8_t)256 = 0. The code read line as uint8_t, giving 40.88 for SI,H instead of the correct 48.27. A new int16_t column version was built from pre.csv preserving full values (range 1-482). Category E fix: mmap line_int16.bin, read as int16_t, treat 0 as null. Performance root cause (Category B): the iter_0 code allocates 64 per-thread ThreadState objects each containing bitset<86135>[64] ≈ 685KB, totaling ~44MB of page-faulted memory outside any timed phase, adding ~33ms overhead on top of a 9ms actual scan. Fix: replace per-thread bitsets with a single shared atomic uint64_t bitset (14 groups × 1347 words = 150KB); per-thread state shrinks to 336 bytes (always cache-resident). Expected result: correctness fixed; hot time should drop from 42ms to ~10-12ms by eliminating the large allocation overhead.
